message.content: Adversaries may exploit the extensible development features of enterprise server applications to establish persistent access by installing malicious software components. These features, intended for legitimate functionality extensions, can be abused to embed harmful scripts or software, thereby compromising the server. The impact includes unauthorized access, data exfiltration, and potential disruption of services. Common exploitation methods involve leveraging server application APIs, plugins, or custom scripts to introduce malicious code. Mitigation strategies include regular auditing of installed components, restricting installation permissions to trusted administrators, employing application whitelisting, and monitoring server logs for unusual activities. Ensuring that server software is up-to-date and applying security patches promptly can also reduce the risk of exploitation.
